headline: Higher Education Funding Per Student Falls 28% Since 2015
context: Real per-student government funding for public universities declined 28% from 2015 to 2025 after adjusting for inflation.

cards.big: Government spends RM18,200 per public university student in 2025 versus RM25,300 in 2015
cards.sub: Inflation-adjusted decline forces universities to cut services and raise fees

cards.big: Operating grants for 20 public universities totaled RM6.8 billion in 2025
cards.sub: In 2015 the same institutions received RM8.1 billion in current terms

cards.big: University of Malaya raised ancillary fees by 45% between 2020 and 2025
cards.sub: Hidden cost increases shift burden from government budgets to student pockets

cards.big: OECD average public spending per tertiary student is USD12,300 in 2024
cards.sub: Malaysia's equivalent is approximately USD4,100 or one-third the OECD level

cards.big: Cutting university funding while demanding world-class rankings is a mathematical impossibility
Should Malaysia set a minimum per-student funding floor indexed to inflation
